{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the pure histological landmark for the termination of cleaning, shaping, and obturation. The documents do not provide direct information about the histological landmarks, but they discuss the apical foramen and apical constriction in relation to endodontic procedures. The apical foramen is the opening of the root canal at the apex of the root, and the apical constriction is the narrowest part of the root canal near the apical foramen. These landmarks are crucial in endodontic procedures as they mark the end of the root canal. Therefore, the apical foramen and apical constriction are both relevant histological landmarks for the termination of these procedures.", "answer_choice": "D"}
